name: class-validator
what_it_does: TypeScript/JavaScript validation library using decorators. class-validator uses property decorators (@IsString, @IsEmail, @IsNotEmpty, etc.) on class definitions to declare validation rules. Works with class-transformer for transforming plain JSON objects to class instances before validation. The standard validation approach for NestJS and TypeORM-based agent backends.
use_cases: Validate agent API request DTOs in NestJS backends with decorator-based validation rules
Define data transfer objects for agent APIs with TypeScript type safety and runtime validation
Validate configuration objects for agent services with nested object and array validation
Combine with class-transformer in NestJS pipes for automatic request body transformation and validation
Create reusable validation groups for agent workflows where different operations require different field subsets
not_for: Non-class-based (functional) TypeScript — use Zod or Valibot for schema-based validation
Projects not using decorators — requires TypeScript experimental decorators and reflect-metadata
Frontend-only applications — Zod or Valibot have better tree-shaking for browser bundles
best_when: You're building NestJS agent backends with TypeORM and want decorator-based validation on DTO classes that integrates with NestJS's validation pipe.
avoid_when: You're not using NestJS or TypeScript decorators — use Zod for schema-based validation with better type inference.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ValidationError array with property, constraints, and children fields. Nested errors in children array. Errors are structured and programmatically queryable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Validation is deterministic — same object always produces same validation result.
known_agent_gotchas: requires reflect-metadata to be imported ONCE at application entry point — missing import causes all decorators to silently fail validation
Must enable experimentalDecorators and emitDecoratorMetadata in tsconfig.json — without these, type metadata is not available for type-based validators
Plain objects (from JSON.parse) must be transformed to class instances with class-transformer before validation — validating plain objects skips decorator checks
Nested object validation requires @ValidateNested() + @Type(() => NestedClass) — without @Type, nested objects are not recursively validated
validate() is async, validateSync() is sync — using validateSync in async contexts works but validateSync may be slower for complex schemas
Custom validators using @ValidatorConstraint must be registered as providers in NestJS — missing injection causes runtime errors on first validation

security_score_components.security_notes: Validation is the security boundary — define comprehensive validation rules to prevent malformed agent inputs from reaching business logic.
